content:
Given the undirected dynamic graph with the edges [(0, 1, 0), (1, 2, 1), (0, 2, 2), (1, 6, 2), (3, 7, 4), (2, 3, 6), (2, 9, 9), (0, 8, 12), (1, 8, 13), (6, 7, 14), (4, 9, 19), (4, 8, 27), (5, 7, 28), (5, 9, 28), (4, 5, 29)], we need to find when node 1 and node 6 are first connected.

We will scan each edge and collect the time t whenever u and v match the two queried nodes (in either order).

First, we have (0, 1, 0) and (1, 2, 1). We will not consider (0, 2, 2) as it does not involve the queried nodes.

Next, we have (1, 6, 2) and (3, 7, 4). We will not consider (2, 3, 6) as it does not involve the queried nodes.

Then, we have (2, 9, 9) and (0, 8, 12). We will not consider (1, 8, 13) as it does not involve the queried nodes.

Furthermore, we have (6, 7, 14) and (4, 9, 19). We will not consider (5, 7, 28) as it does not involve the queried nodes.

Lastly, we have (4, 8, 27) and (5, 9, 28). We will not consider (4, 5, 29) as it does not involve the queried nodes.

After scanning all edges, we have found that node 1 and node 6 are first connected at time 2.

Answer: 2
